title: Present Tense Patterns
description: Learn the regular present tense by looking at the person endings, the common verb groups, and the forms that stay stable across many verbs.

content.title: The main present-tense groups
content.description: Bulgarian verbs are usually taught in groups based on their infinitive stem and the ending they take in the dictionary form.

content.title: Focus on person endings first
content.text: The ending usually matters more than the dictionary label. Once you know how 1st, 2nd, and 3rd person behave, most regular verbs become predictable.

content.title: Person endings
content.description: Use one stable model to recognize who is doing the action.

content.title: How to read the pattern
bullets: Check whether the stem changes before the ending.
Compare the singular and plural forms to see which vowels stay stable.
Use context when a form can belong to more than one verb group.

content.title: Examples in sentences
content.description: These examples show how present tense sounds in natural speech.

content.question: Which form is 1st person singular in the present tense for чета?
content.note: The dictionary form and the 1st person singular are often the same for this group.
